question: At about what air tank pressure does the governor stop the compressor from pumping air (cut-out)?
explanation: When tank pressure rises to the cut-out level (around 125 psi), the governor stops the compressor from pumping air.

question: At about what tank pressure does the governor allow the compressor to start pumping again (cut-in)?
explanation: When tank pressure falls to the cut-in pressure (around 100 psi), the governor allows the compressor to start pumping again.

question: At what pressure is the safety relief valve usually set to open?
explanation: The safety relief valve is usually set to open at 150 psi to protect the system from too much pressure.

question: A visible low air pressure warning signal must come on before tank pressure falls below what level?
explanation: A warning signal you can see must come on before the air pressure in the tanks falls below 60 psi.

question: At what pressure does a wig wag device drop a mechanical arm into the driver's view?
explanation: The wig wag drops a mechanical arm into your view when system pressure drops below 60 psi.

question: Tractor and straight truck spring brakes come fully on when air pressure drops to what range?
explanation: Tractor and straight truck spring brakes will come fully on when air pressure drops to a range of 20 to 45 psi (typically 20 to 30 psi).

question: Automatic front wheel limiting valves stop reducing air to the front brakes when application pressure reaches about what level?
explanation: Automatic front wheel limiting valves reduce air to the front brakes except when the brakes are applied very hard (60 psi or more application pressure).

question: Before driving a dual air system vehicle, the compressor should build a minimum of what pressure in both systems?
explanation: Allow time for the air compressor to build up a minimum of 100 psi pressure in both the primary and secondary systems before driving.

question: In a dual air brake system, the warning light and buzzer should come on before air pressure drops below what level in either system?
explanation: The warning light and buzzer should come on before the air pressure drops below 60 psi in either system.

question: When pulling on a slack adjuster, about how much movement indicates it probably needs adjustment?
choices: More than about one quarter inch
More than about one half inch
explanation: If a slack adjuster moves more than about one inch where the push rod attaches, it probably needs adjustment.

question: Brake drums or discs must not have cracks longer than what fraction of the friction area width?
explanation: Brake drums or discs must not have cracks longer than one half the width of the friction area.

topic: Leakage rate engine off, no brake
question: With the engine off and parking brake released, the air loss rate should be less than what for a single vehicle?
explanation: The loss rate should be less than two psi in one minute for single vehicles and less than three psi for combination vehicles.

question: With the foot brake fully applied for one minute, the air pressure should not drop more than what for a single vehicle?
explanation: With the foot brake fully applied, air pressure dropping more than three psi in one minute (single vehicle) means the air loss rate is too much.

question: During the air brake check, the parking brake valve should pop out causing spring brakes to come on when pressure falls into what range?
explanation: The parking brake valve should close (pop out) and spring brakes come on when air pressure falls to the manufacturer's specification of 20 to 45 psi.

question: In dual air systems at operating rpm, pressure should build from 85 to 100 psi within how long?
explanation: In dual air systems, pressure should build from 85 to 100 psi within 45 seconds when the engine is at operating rpms.

question: When using stab braking, when should you reapply the brakes after releasing them?
choices: As soon as the wheels start rolling again
After holding the release for five seconds
Only after the vehicle fully straightens
explanation: In stab braking you release brakes when wheels lock up and reapply them fully as soon as the wheels start rolling again.

question: About how much distance does air brake lag add at 55 mph on dry pavement?
explanation: The air brake lag distance at 55 mph on dry pavement adds about 32 feet.

question: Using proper braking technique on a downgrade, when should you release the brakes after slowing?
choices: When speed is reduced to about 5 mph below the safe speed
When speed reaches the safe speed exactly
When speed is 10 mph above the safe speed
explanation: Release the brakes when your speed has been reduced to approximately five mph below your safe speed.
